text: To manage a team effectively, start with clear communication. Make sure everyone understands their tasks and the team’s goals. Delegation is also important—don’t try to do everything yourself. Assign tasks based on each person’s strengths. Provide regular feedback to help team members improve and feel valued. This also boosts motivation. When conflicts arise, address them quickly. Listen to both sides and find a fair solution. By focusing on these areas, you can build a strong, productive team where everyone works well together.
